item.title: A partner who plays music
item.description: pemDrafted March 6 2025/em/ppspanI have thought for many years that it is a risky thing to have a partner who is into creating things, lest you be called upon to appreciate those things, and everyone’s preferred narrative about the situation slams head-on into the realistic odds that you especially

item.title: Can we use steering vectors to suppress reward-hacking? Somewhat
item.description: pCan steering vectors drive gradient routing? Yes, but not in realisticreward hacking environments, they are not precise enough classifiers ofhacky vs clean solutions./ppInstead, can we use a steering vector to initialise adapters so that gradient routing happens without a classifier, and we get aut

item.title: BeamGPT: A new paradigm for attention
item.description: pspanI have found an operator that achieves striking results in learning curves when used alongside standard attention in a nanoGPT-style character-level language model. It finds structure in the sequence that attention misses./span/pimg alt="image.png" src="https://res.cloudinary.com/lesswrong-2-0/

item.title: Tommy explains how AI workloads could split between open source and frontier models. “My mental model is like 10% of requests should go to a frontier model, 90% should go to open source.”

item.title: Do LLMs Have Desires?
item.description: pispanWork conducted with Yujun Zhou (yzhou25@nd.edu) and supported by /span/ia href="https://sparai.org/" rel="noreferrer"ispanSPAR/span/i/a/ppbspanTL;DR:/span/b/pulli value="1"spanIn paired-choice paradigms, LLMs report consistent preferences over outcomes (e.g., types and number of lives saved, t

item.title: Hawaii is turning ocean plastic and fishing nets into roads
item.description: Hawaii researchers are giving old fishing nets and recycled plastic a second life by mixing them into asphalt roads. Early tests found these roads didn't release more plastic particles than standard pavement, with tire wear overwhelming any plastic signal from the recycled material. If future studie

item.title: Some subtypes of taskishness / corrigibility
item.description: p"Corrigibility" is somewhat of an overloaded term in alignment - it points in the direction of a cluster of desirable properties, but different people have different ideas of what this entails./ppI think of "corrigibility", as it is used, to cover a few different ideas. I will name some of these an

item.title: Agents as Webs of Beliefs
item.description: pspanIn this post I’ll sketch out an informal model of intelligent agents as webs of beliefs (or belief webs for short). The belief webs framework pulls together ideas from active inference, agent foundations and machine learning. In doing so it aims to unify beliefs, goals and actions as three face

item.title: The Prisoner's Dilemma (Fiction)
item.description: pspanHis hands are shaking. They’ve been shaking since five hours ago, when the silent policemen showed up at his door wearing black tactical vests on top of black sleeves. He lived alone—there was no one to call for, no one to beg to call his lawyer as they took him into the sealed van. The officer

item.title: Neuralese is Actually Probably Good for Alignment
item.description: pThe best language models are still getting smarter and more capable. To an increasing degree, this is because they are trained by Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards. Chain of thought reasoning allows models to evade the finite depth restriction on information flow by passing (relatively

item.title: New FBI Alert: Russian Intelligence Uses Signal Recovery Keys to Access Messages https://securityaffairs.com/194360/intelligence/new-fbi-alert-russian-intelligence-uses-signal-recovery-keys-to-access-
item.description: pNew FBI Alert: Russian Intelligence Uses Signal Recovery Keys to Access Messages a href="https://securityaffairs.com/194360/intelligence/new-fbi-alert-russian-intelligence-uses-signal-recovery-keys-to-access-messages.html"securityaffairs.com/194360/i…/a/phr /bLink/bbr /a href="https://securityaffai

item.title: AMLBot Puts Polymarket Phishing Toll at $3.1M Across 11 Wallets, Funds Traced to Ethereum
item.description: Blockchain intelligence firm AMLBot has confirmed the Polymarket supply-chain attack total at approximately $3.1 million in PUSD across 11 user wallets, with funds bridged from Polygon to Ethereum and converted to ETH. Polymarket has pledged full refunds but has not named the compromised vendor.

item.title: Austin & Oli on funding and incubating projects
item.description: pa href="https://www.lesswrong.com/users/habryka4?mention=user"span@habryka/span/aspan and I recently spoke about his plans to improve the AI safety funding ecosystem with a better /spana href="https://survivalandflourishing.fund/s-process" rel="noreferrer"spanS-Process/span/aspan platform, and my n

item.title: Aave Confirms Aavenomics 3.0 Is Live With Buybacks and DAO Spending Cut
item.description: Aave confirmed Saturday that Aavenomics 3.0 is now active, with automated AAVE token buybacks running and DAO operational spending reduced, completing a governance roadmap the protocol has built toward since mid-2024.

item.title: Strategy's Enterprise mNAV Drops Below 1 for the First Time
item.description: Strategy's enterprise market-to-NAV ratio crossed below 1 on Thursday for the first time, putting the combined weight of its debt, preferred stock, and equity above the value of its 847,363-bitcoin treasury and closing the equity-accretion channel that powered its buying spree.

item.title: Scientists discover what triggers belly fat as we age
item.description: Aging may trigger the appearance of specialized stem cells that supercharge the body's ability to create new belly fat. The discovery reveals a potential biological driver of middle-age weight gain and a promising target for future anti-obesity treatments.

item.title: Clean GitHub repo tricks AI coding agents into running malware https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/clean-github-repo-tricks-ai-coding-agents-into-running-malware/
